heroSub: The story of Fankaar Institute of Performing Arts, Jaipur.
story: Fankaar began with a simple conviction: that India's classical arts are not museum pieces but living practices — and that anyone with a zeal to learn deserves a guru who takes them seriously. What started as a handful of Kathak students in Jaipur has grown into an institute of four branches and an online classroom, with more than 175 students trained and three CCRT scholarship awardees among them.

The name is the mission. Fankaar (फ़नकार) means artist — and every class, from a six-year-old's first tatkaar to an adult learner's Alankaar preparation, is designed to awaken that artist. We train for depth, not display: correct technique, living repertoire, and the discipline that makes creative freedom possible.
mission: Fankaar exists to preserve the purity and timelessness of India's classical and folk performing arts — guiding every student, through mentorship and discipline, toward the creative freedom that awakens the fankaar (artist) within.

founder.name: Vartika Tiwari
founder.bio: Vartika Tiwari: Leading Kathak Dancer, Research Scholar, and Founder of Fankaar ArtsVartika Tiwari is a distinguished Indian classical dancer, choreographer, and elite Kathak exponent based in Jaipur, Rajasthan. With over 20 years of intensive training in the traditional Indian classical dance form, she is widely recognized for her deep mastery of the Jaipur Gharana style of Kathak. As the visionary Founder and Artistic Director of Fankaar- Center for Performing Arts, she has successfully expanded the institute into a premier multi-branch classical academy, nurturing over 175 students across four prominent locations in Jaipur.Pristine Classical Lineage and TrainingVartika’s foundational artistry was molded under the legendary Pt. Girdhari Maharaj of the Jaipur Gharana and subsequently refined under the guidance of the eminent Guru Ishira Parikh (p. 1). Dedicated to preserving the authentic Guru-Shishya tradition, her performances blend intricate footwork, rapid pirouettes, and highly expressive storytelling.Academic Excellence & Research BackgroundVartika stands at the forefront of classical dance research and education in India, holding a robust academic profile:Ph.D. Research Scholar (Kathak): Currently conducting doctoral research under the mentorship of the revered Dr. Puru Dhadheech.Master of Performing Arts (Kathak): Graduated from Gujarat University.Triple Diploma Credentials: Earned recognized classical diplomas from Bhatkhande University (Lucknow), Gandharva Mahavidyalaya (Mumbai), and Rajasthan Sangeet Sansthan (Jaipur).Prestigious Awards and RecognitionsIn 2018, her exceptional dedication to the performing arts was recognized at the national level when she was awarded the highly selective CCRT (Center for Cultural Resources & Training) Scholarship by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India (p. 1).Founder of Fankaar Arts, JaipurIn 2021, Vartika established Fankaar—an institute that began with just four students and has rapidly grown to encompass a vibrant community of passionate learners (p. 1). Operating out of Malviya Nagar, Tonk Road, Jagatpura, and Nirman Nagar, Fankaar remains the premier destination for students of all age groups looking to clear certified board examinations or learn classical dance and Hindustani music in an authentic, supportive environment.

approach.title: Guru-shishya parampara
approach.text: Small batches and close mentorship — every student is seen, corrected and encouraged personally, the way classical arts have always been passed on.

approach.title: A structured path
approach.text: Training is mapped to recognised exam stages — Prarambhik, Praveshika, Visharad — through boards like Bhatkhande Sanskriti Vishwavidyalaya, Gandharva Mahavidyalaya and Rajasthan Sangeet Sansthan.

approach.title: The stage as classroom
approach.text: Recitals, showcases and community performances are part of the curriculum. Students learn to carry their art into the world with poise.
